papers.summary_cn: 这篇论文讨论当前大语言模型在跨推理会话上天然无状态，许多更高层认知能力只能在应用层通过提示工程和上下文管理来模拟。作者提出一个理论性元架构框架，希望把这些应用层协议下沉到模型原生层面。框架由结构张力、离线递归循环和推理时可塑性三部分组成，并强调可审计、可逆和连续性的治理约束。
innovations: 提出“结构张力”机制，将新信息与既有拓扑之间的冲突视为驱动内部自洽演化的内生信号。
引入“离线递归循环”，设想模型可在无外部输入时进行受控的自处理与冲突消化。
提出“推理时可塑性”概念，强调在不修改预训练参数的前提下重构上下文流形拓扑。
将治理而非单纯能力作为架构智能的重要判据，并给出操作性定义、重构算子与可证伪标准。
papers.scenario_cn: 适用于研究具备持续内部状态、记忆循环和受治理自我重构能力的下一代智能体架构，尤其适合讨论长期记忆、元认知控制和异构智能演化的理论设计场景。
papers.title_en: From Application-Layer Simulation to Native Meta-Architecture: Structural Tension as an Endogenous Driver for Heterogeneous AI Evolution

papers.title: MoWorld: A Flash World Model
papers.abstract: The future of World Models depends not only on scaling model capability, but also on scaling practicality and inference efficiency. High-frame-rate inference enables responsive perception, planning, and control in real-world autonomous systems. To this end, we present MoWorld, a cost-effective yet high-performance Flash World Model with an end-to-end framework spanning data generation, pre-training, distillation, and efficient inference, enabling up to 50 FPS real-time interaction with cinematic visual quality without the need of high-end GPUs. To enable large-scale real-world deployment, MoWorld jointly optimizes model capability and cost throughout the entire development pipeline. Specifically, unlike existing approaches that primarily rely on large-scale video corpora, MoWorld is built upon a scalable 3D-native data engine accumulated from our large-scale 3D vision and generative modeling pipeline, enabling the efficient construction of geometrically consistent training data across diverse real-world and synthetic environments. Based on this foundation, a curriculum cross-frame pre-training strategy for stable and scalable World Model learning, an efficient denoising-step distillation algorithm to reduce diffusion training cost, and a mixed-precision parallel inference framework for low-cost real-time deployment. MoWorld is the first real-time interactive World Model built on the Neural Processing Unit (NPU) and can achieves up to 50 FPS in such the devices, enabling practical and efficient deployment at scale. Comprehensive evaluations demonstrate that MoWorld achieves leading performance; notably, its average inference cost is only 30\%-50\% of that of existing World Models, providing a practical foundation for large-scale real-world applications of World Models. We also demonstrate diverse applications of MoWorld.

papers.summary_cn: MoWorld 提出了一种同时强调性能、成本与推理效率的实时交互式世界模型。论文覆盖从数据生成、预训练、蒸馏到高效推理的端到端方案，目标是在不依赖高端 GPU 的情况下实现高帧率交互。作者还强调其基于 3D 原生数据引擎、课程式跨帧预训练、去噪步蒸馏和混合精度并行推理，并称其可在 NPU 上实现最高 50 FPS、显著降低平均推理成本。
innovations: 提出面向世界模型的端到端工程框架，联合优化数据、训练、蒸馏与部署全链路成本。
使用可扩展的 3D 原生数据引擎构建几何一致的真实与合成环境训练数据，而非主要依赖大规模视频语料。
设计课程式跨帧预训练策略，以支持更稳定、可扩展的世界模型学习。
提出高效去噪步蒸馏与混合精度并行推理框架，面向低成本实时部署，并强调 NPU 上的实时交互能力。
papers.scenario_cn: 面向自动驾驶、机器人、交互式仿真与需要实时感知-规划-控制闭环的场景，适合追求高帧率、较低部署成本和大规模落地的世界模型应用。
papers.title_en: MoWorld: A Flash World Model

papers.title: Narrative World Model: Narratology-Grounded Writer Memory for Long-Form Fiction
papers.abstract: Long-form fiction writers need memory that answers multi-hop questions about evolving story state: who knows a secret and when they learned it, whether an event preceded the narration that revealed it, whether a setup paid off, and how a relationship shifted. General-purpose retrieval and agent-memory systems represent entities and facts but not the narratological structure these questions turn on, so they surface the wrong evidence or none at all. We introduce the Narrative World Model (NWM), a writer-memory system that pairs a narratology-grounded typed temporal-state graph with query-conditioned hybrid retrieval. To measure memory rather than the answerer, we read every system through a single held-constant Opus 4.8 reader over only that system's chapter-safe evidence, on a reproducible public corpus and a validated multi-hop benchmark, and we compare against the strongest existing temporal-knowledge-graph agent-memory framework, Graphiti/Zep (Rasmussen et al., 2025). NWM substantially and significantly outperforms this baseline on multi-hop narratological QA across both corpora, and far exceeds GraphRAG and flat retrieval. papers.summary_cn: 这篇论文针对长篇小说写作中的复杂记忆需求，提出了 Narrative World Model（NWM）。作者认为通用检索和 Agent Memory 系统虽然能表示实体与事实，但无法充分表达叙事学结构，因此难以回答“谁何时知道秘密”“伏笔是否回收”“关系如何变化”等多跳问题。NWM 通过基于叙事学的类型化时序状态图和查询条件化混合检索来解决这一问题，并在多跳叙事问答上显著优于 Graphiti/Zep、GraphRAG 和扁平检索。
innovations: 提出面向小说写作记忆的叙事学建模方式，将记忆表示为类型化的时序状态图。
将查询条件化混合检索与叙事结构表示结合，以更好支持多跳叙事问答。
采用固定阅读器和章节安全证据的评测设计，尽量把测量重点放在“记忆系统本身”而非回答器能力上。
通过重建基线并控制抽取器因素，论证性能优势主要来自表示结构与检索机制，而非图规模或抽取质量。
papers.scenario_cn: 适用于长篇小说创作辅助、叙事一致性维护、角色关系追踪和复杂情节回溯等场景，也可用于需要时序叙事理解与多跳检索的创意写作系统。
papers.title_en: Narrative World Model: Narratology-Grounded Writer Memory for Long-Form Fiction

papers.title: CMDR: Contextual Multimodal Document Retrieval
papers.abstract: Multimodal document retrieval aims to retrieve relevant pages while preserving both textual and visual content from the original document. However, existing benchmarks primarily evaluate simple lexical or semantic matching, and most methods encode pages independently. Consequently, they overlook the contextual information in the document required to resolve queries that aggregate information across multiple pages. In this paper, we introduce CMDR and CMDR-Bench, a new multimodal document retrieval task and benchmark that require modeling document context. To address this challenge, we propose CMDR-Embed, a contextual multimodal embedding framework that explicitly incorporates document context by jointly encoding multiple pages and deriving page-level embeddings from a shared contextual representation. Furthermore, we introduce CMCL, a contextual multimodal contrastive learning objective that effectively trains CMDR-Embed by balancing contextual modeling with page-level discriminability. Experiments demonstrate that CMDR-Embed significantly outperforms non-contextual embeddings, highlighting the importance of context-aware multimodal embeddings for advancing document retrieval.

papers.title_zh: CMDR：上下文感知的多模态文档检索
papers.summary_cn: 这篇论文研究多模态文档检索，目标是在检索相关页面时同时保留页面的文本与视觉内容。作者指出现有基准和方法大多按页独立编码，难以处理需要跨页聚合信息的查询，因此提出新的任务与基准 CMDR / CMDR-Bench。为解决这一问题，论文提出 CMDR-Embed，通过联合编码多页文档并从共享上下文表示中导出页级表示，同时配合新的对比学习目标 CMCL 来兼顾上下文建模与页级区分能力。
innovations: 提出需要显式建模文档上下文的多模态文档检索任务与基准 CMDR / CMDR-Bench。
提出 CMDR-Embed，通过联合编码多个页面并利用共享上下文表示生成页级嵌入。
提出 CMCL（上下文化多模态对比学习目标），在上下文建模与页面可区分性之间取得平衡。
强调并验证了上下文感知嵌入在跨页信息聚合型检索中的优势。
papers.scenario_cn: 适用于长文档、报告、教材、表格图文混排材料等多页文档检索场景，特别是查询答案分散在多个页面、必须结合上下文才能定位相关页的时候。它解决的是逐页独立建模忽略文档整体上下文、从而导致跨页检索能力不足的问题。
papers.title_en: CMDR: Contextual Multimodal Document Retrieval

papers.title: Clustering-Embedded Model Predictive Path Integral Control: Avoiding Averaging-Induced Failure and Enabling Efficient Cluster Selection for Dynamic Obstacles
papers.abstract: With the widespread availability of parallel computing hardware, sampling-based motion planning methods such as Model Predictive Path Integral (MPPI) control have become increasingly powerful for complex nonlinear systems in non-smooth task spaces. However, the sampling and forward-simulation pipeline in MPPI suffers from averaging-induced failure in cluttered environments, where the importance-weighted update averages incompatible rollouts and leads to hesitation or even collision when an obstacle lies directly ahead. This paper proposes Clustering-Embedded MPPI (CE-MPPI), a framework that architecturally resolves the averaging-induced failures inherent in standard MPPI within non-convex environments. Rather than simply mitigating interference, CE-MPPI redefines the control law by integrating a high-fidelity pruning and clustering stage. By leveraging density-based spatial clustering of applications with noise (DBSCAN) alongside a novel geometric direction feature that is extracted from collision-derived reference points, the system isolates feasible trajectory modes from the noise of infeasible rollouts. This is paired with an intelligent selection logic that optimizes for minimum cost in static scenes while actively steering opposite to obstacle flux in dynamic environments. Experiments in 2-D JAX-accelerated simulations show that CE-MPPI alleviates obstacle-front hesitation and avoids persistent coupling with moving obstacles in dynamic scenes. In particular, real-world tests on a 6-DoF UR5e manipulator with CUDA-parallel rollouts in Isaac Gym achieve a 48\% reduction in time-to-goal and a 12\% shorter end-effector path.

papers.title_zh: 聚类嵌入式模型预测路径积分控制：避免平均化诱发失效并实现面向动态障碍的高效簇选择
papers.summary_cn: 这篇论文研究采样式运动规划中的 MPPI 控制在拥挤、非凸环境下的失效问题。作者指出，标准 MPPI 的重要性加权更新会对彼此不兼容的轨迹进行平均，从而在正前方有障碍物时产生犹豫甚至碰撞。为此论文提出 CE-MPPI，在控制流程中加入高保真裁剪与聚类阶段，从采样轨迹中分离可行轨迹模式，并结合静态/动态场景下的选择逻辑来决定控制更新方向。
innovations: 指出并针对性解决标准 MPPI 在非凸障碍环境中的 averaging-induced failure（平均化诱发失效）问题。
提出 CE-MPPI，将高保真裁剪与聚类步骤直接嵌入控制律，而不仅是做后处理补救。
结合 DBSCAN 与基于碰撞参考点提取的新几何方向特征，以区分可行轨迹模式与不可行噪声轨迹。
设计面向静态和动态障碍的簇选择逻辑：静态场景偏向最小代价，动态场景考虑避开障碍物流向。
papers.scenario_cn: 适用于机器人运动规划、机械臂避障、动态障碍环境导航等需要实时采样控制的场景。它主要解决标准 MPPI 在复杂障碍前因轨迹平均造成的犹豫、碰撞风险以及与移动障碍持续耦合的问题。
papers.title_en: Clustering-Embedded Model Predictive Path Integral Control: Avoiding Averaging-Induced Failure and Enabling Efficient Cluster Selection for Dynamic Obstacles

papers.title: Code-Level Cost Function Generation for Spatial Image Steganography Using RAG-Enhanced Large Language Models
papers.abstract: Designing cost functions of adaptive steganography traditionally requires extensive manual tuning, while deep learning methods lack interpretability. Although large language models (LLMs) offer an automated alternative via evolutionary generation, they often violate domain specific mathematical constraints due to a lack of explicit domain knowledge. To address this problem, we propose a novel evolutionary system focused on exploiting Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) enhanced LLMs for the automatic code-level generation of spatial steganography cost functions. This system incorporates a core Self Evolving RAG (SE-RAG) module, wherein a Code Semantic Signature (CSS) translates procedural code into aligned queries, retrieving explicit guidance from static literature and dynamic experience knowledge bases to steer the LLM generation process. A dedicated feedback mechanism then continuously refines the dynamic knowledge base with successful optimization strategies. Extensive experiments on the BOSSBase and BOWS2 datasets demonstrate that the proposed framework consistently achieves higher steganographic security than existing automatically designed methods, and increases the average code execution rate by 46.3% while reducing the search cost by 26.1%, thereby highlighting the effectiveness, efficiency, and potential of combining LLMs with domain-specific knowledge in the field of automatic steganographic algorithm generation.

papers.content_snippet: Designing cost functions of adaptive steganography traditionally requires extensive manual tuning, while deep learning methods lack interpretability. Although large language models (LLMs) offer an automated alternative via evolutionary generation, they often violate domain specific mathematical constraints due to a lack of explicit domain knowledge. To address this problem, we propose a novel evolutionary system focused on exploiting Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) enhanced LLMs for the automatic code-level generation of spatial steganography cost functions. This system incorporates a core Self Evolving RAG (SE-RAG) module, wherein a Code Semantic Signature (CSS) translates procedural code into aligned queries, retrieving explicit guidance from static literature and dynamic experience knowledge bases to steer the LLM generation process. A dedicated feedback mechanism then continuously refines the dynamic knowledge base with successful optimization strategies. Extensive experiments on the BOSSBase and BOWS2 datasets demonstrate that the proposed framework consistently achieves higher steganographic security than existing automatically designed methods, and increases the average code execution rate by 46.3% while reducing the search cost by 26.1%, thereby highlighting the effectiveness, efficiency, and potential of combining LLMs with domain-specific knowledge in the field of automatic steganographic algorithm generation.
papers.title_zh: 利用 RAG 增强大语言模型进行空间图像隐写代码级代价函数生成
papers.summary_cn: 这篇论文研究自适应图像隐写中的代价函数自动设计问题。作者指出，传统方法依赖人工调参，而直接用大语言模型做进化式生成又容易违反领域数学约束，因此提出一个基于 RAG 增强 LLM 的代码级自动生成框架。其核心是 Self Evolving RAG（SE-RAG）模块：先用代码语义签名把过程代码转成检索查询，从静态文献库和动态经验库中取回指导信息，再通过反馈机制持续更新经验库以改进后续生成。
innovations: 提出面向空间图像隐写代价函数的代码级自动生成框架，而非仅生成文本描述或黑盒模型。
设计 SE-RAG，将静态文献知识与动态经验知识结合起来约束和引导 LLM 生成。
提出 Code Semantic Signature（CSS），把过程代码转化为与知识库对齐的检索查询。
引入反馈机制，利用成功的优化策略持续更新动态知识库，实现自演化改进。
papers.scenario_cn: 适用于自适应图像隐写算法设计与自动优化场景，尤其是在需要兼顾生成效率、可解释性和领域约束时。它要解决的是人工设计代价函数成本高，以及通用 LLM 在缺少显式领域知识时容易生成不满足隐写约束代码的问题。
papers.title_en: Code-Level Cost Function Generation for Spatial Image Steganography Using RAG-Enhanced Large Language Models
